rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks into valid component or implementation scopes
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use visible identifiers when available; otherwise, use valid component type features for contracts
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations and comparisons
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks at the appropriate scope level to maintain architectural integrity
Use valid feature names and visible identifiers when available for contracts
Avoid mixing implementation-specific details with component type contracts
Ensure type consistency in declarations and comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contract expressions
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not mix component type and implementation-level bindings in the same annex
Do not use opaque Boolean data in range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
